The bioactive constituents of Cassia hispida include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Cassia hispida is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as in traditional medicine, where it is valued for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and analgesic effects.
